blocks.statement: I can distinguish mass from weight, calculate weight on different worlds with F = mg, and explain why weight changes but mass does not.

blocks.whatWeKnow: 2026-XJ's mass is the same everywhere. Its weight depends on WHERE it is.
blocks.connection: Set up the question: if 2026-XJ stood on Earth's surface, what would it weigh? Tomorrow we get the equation that gives g on any world.

blocks.markdown:
## Mass is what you are. Weight is what gravity does to you.

- **Mass** (kg) — how much matter is in an object. The same on Earth, on the Moon, on 2026-XJ.
- **Weight** (N) — the **force** of gravity pulling on that mass: **F_w = m·g**.

Change worlds and you change g — so your weight changes. Your mass never does.

| World | g (m/s²) |
|---|---|
| Earth | 9.81 |
| Moon | 1.62 |
| Mars | 3.71 |

terms.definition: How much MATTER is in an object. Same everywhere in the universe. Measured in kg.
terms.cognate: Sp. masa · Pt. massa · HC mas

terms.definition: The FORCE of gravity pulling on a mass. Depends on local g. A 70 kg student weighs 686 N on Earth, 113 N on the Moon, 260 N on Mars.
terms.cognate: Sp. peso · Pt. peso · HC pwa

blocks.title: Cognate alert
blocks.markdown: *peso* = weight (Sp.), *masa* = mass (Sp.). Don't mix them up. Your bathroom scale measures **force** (weight), not 'how much stuff' (mass).

blocks.prompt: A 70 kg student stands on the Moon. What is their weight? (g_Moon = 1.62 m/s²)
blocks.answer: 113 N. About 1/6 their Earth weight, but their mass is unchanged.

blocks.patternPrompt: Pick your own mass (be honest). Fill in your weight on Earth, the Moon, Mars, and one world of your choice. What pattern connects g and your weight?

blocks.prompt: If you weigh 600 N on Earth, find your mass first. Then calculate your weight on Mars (g_Mars = 3.71 m/s²).
blocks.givenHint: Earth weight (600 N), g_Earth = 9.81 m/s², g_Mars = 3.71 m/s².
blocks.equationHint: Use F_w = m·g twice: once backwards to get m, once forwards on Mars.

blocks.prompt: If you weigh 600 N on Earth, what is your mass? What would you weigh on a planet with g = 25 m/s²?
blocks.frame: My mass is ___ kg, because mass = weight ÷ ___. On the g = 25 planet I would weigh ___ N.
